$schema: Branding spec for RL Clubhouse VIP. Read by the ads agent before generating image creative or writing copy. Source of truth — when /vip changes visually, update this file.

brand.name: RL Clubhouse
brand.tagline: Rank up in 6 weeks. Or we coach you free until you do.
brand.subTagline: 1-on-1 with a pro coach, a personalized 6-week plan, and a guarantee most coaches won't make.

background.description: Deep navy-black. Use as canvas for static ads. Never pure black.

accent.description: Indigo-purple. Used on CTA buttons, ring highlights, the live spots chip pulse. Primary visual identifier.

accentGlow.description: Soft halo around CTAs. Use sparingly in ad creative — one focal point per image.

secondary.description: Used in bottom radial gradient. Background depth, not foreground.

live.description: Emerald green. Used on the live-spots pulse only. In ads: scarcity / activity signal (e.g., 'spots filling now').

display.usage: Headlines. Tight tracking, slight negative letter-spacing. Use for big-text overlays in ads.

body.usage: All body copy, subheads, supporting text.

uppercase.usage: Spots chip and section eyebrows only ('STEP 1 OF 2', '47 OF 60 FOUNDING SPOTS FILLED'). Strong tracking, small size. Use in ads for category labels or scarcity callouts.

visualMotifs.spotsChip: Pill-shaped chip with green pulsing dot and uppercase tracking-widest text. The signature scarcity motif. Re-use in static ads ('13 SPOTS LEFT', '47 OF 60 FILLED').
visualMotifs.radialGradients: Two radial gradients — purple top, deeper purple bottom — over near-black canvas. Gives the page its 'serious arena' feel.
visualMotifs.roundedFull: All CTA buttons are fully rounded (pill). Soft modern feel, not gaming-aggressive.
visualMotifs.ringHighlights: Accent-color rings around scarcity/highlight elements (ring-1 with 40% opacity).

voice.tone: Direct, confident, grown. We're talking to adults about a real investment in something they care about. Wry humor okay. Never hype-cringe.
do: Lead with the promise ('Rank up in 6 weeks')
Name the guarantee plainly ('Or we coach you free until you do')
Use rank-specific language (Plat, Diamond, Champ, MMR)
Speak to identity ('serious players', 'people who actually want to rank up')
Use real numbers (47/60 spots, 6 weeks, 13 days, +116 MMR)
Use direct second-person ('You', 'Your Champ logo in 30 days')
dont: Promise 'GC in a month' or other ridiculous outcomes
Sound like a kids' game-coaching ad
Use stock 'gaming' words: epic, legendary, dominate, crush, destroy
Use emoji in copy (page doesn't use them — ads shouldn't either)

EVER mention the price ($497) in any ad creative, headline, primary text, or caption. Price stays inside the funnel — never on a Facebook image that could be screenshotted to Reddit. This rule is absolute.
Tease the price with proxies ('high-ticket', '$500-level coaching', 'premium investment') — same exposure risk
Use the word 'application' or 'qualify' as gatekeeping language in ads — keep it inviting at the top, qualify on the page
exampleHeadlines: Stuck in Plat for 18 months? You're not bad. You don't have a plan.
Hard-stuck Diamond 2? It's not your aim.
Rank up in 6 weeks. Or we coach you free until you do.
What if a pro player built your training plan instead of YouTube?
Snizz: Champ 2 in 13 days. Real client.

logos.primary: VipBrandNav component on /vip — wordmark RL Clubhouse with VIP locked badge
logos.usage: Place top-left or top-center of static ads. Don't stretch, don't recolor, don't add drop shadows.

imageGuidelines.general: Two valid creative sources: (1) ship existing SpookLuke thumbnails AS-IS — proven, recognizable, zero edit overhead, copy lives in Meta's primary text + headline fields. (2) Use Higgsfield / GPT image 2 to GENERATE TEXT AND COMPOSITION around existing realistic RL scenes — the AI handles typography, layout, badges, and design elements; the underlying scene (Octane, rank emblem, stadium) comes from our library or is generated to match that style. NEVER hand-burn text onto a thumbnail — the design comes out amateur. Either ship the thumbnail clean and put copy in Meta metadata, OR let Higgsfield compose a designed variant.
imageGuidelines.preferredVisualLanguage: Saturated, high-contrast RL renders. Rank emblems (Plat, Diamond, Champ, GC logos) as a focal element. Chains, locks, glows, and other 'stuck' / 'unlock' visual metaphors. Dramatic lighting on the Octane/Fennec/Dominus. Dynamic action poses. This is the visual vernacular RL viewers recognize — every Higgsfield prompt should reinforce this style.
imageGuidelines.useExistingLibrary: D:\Mega Cloud Sync\Content Master Folder\SpookLuke Stuff\(9) - Cinematics + Assets + Graphics\Assets - Graphics + Pics\Thumbnails + Headshots\Thumbnails\Finished Thumbnails — organized by year (2020-2026). Hundreds of options. Use as ship-as-is primary creative, OR as the visual style reference fed into Higgsfield prompts ('match the saturated chained-Octane aesthetic of 2023/Champ Hardstuck Plain.jpg').
highPriorityThemes: 'Hardstuck' visuals — rank logos with locks/chains (e.g., 'Champ Hardstuck Plain', 'Champ Arrow Hardstuck')
Rank-up timeline visuals (e.g., 'Diamond in 30 Days', 'Speedrun to GC')
Mistake / mechanic-fix thumbnails (e.g., 'How to Position in Platinum', '7 Mechanics')
Coaching-context thumbnails (e.g., 'I Coached Every Rank', 'Coach Luke' series)

higgsfieldUsage.when: When we want a designed static with prominent headline text on it that the thumbnail library doesn't cover. Especially: copy-heavy variants, headline-first scroll-stoppers, rank progression infographics, comparison cards, and Remy-style 'text + scene' compositions.
higgsfieldUsage.approach: Feed Higgsfield the realistic RL scene reference (either a library thumbnail as image input, or a prompt that matches our visual language). Let it compose the text layout and any design elements (badges, locks, arrows, callouts). The output should look like one of our thumbnails — same saturation, same vibe — but with prominent designed headline text.
higgsfieldUsage.modelChoice: GPT image 2 (via Higgsfield) is the preferred model — handles logos accurately and keeps text rendering crisp. Remy validated this for static ad outputs.
higgsfieldUsage.promptPattern: Static ad in the visual style of a SpookLuke YouTube thumbnail: saturated RL stadium scene, [SPECIFIC SCENE — e.g. 'pink Octane with chained Champ-rank emblem']. Bold sans-serif headline in white with thick black outline, centered on the upper third: '[HEADLINE — e.g. STUCK IN CHAMP?]'. Subline below in smaller text: '[SUBLINE]'. High-contrast lighting, dramatic mood. No watermarks.

avoid: Manually burning text onto an existing thumbnail with a basic image editor — design will look amateur. Either ship clean OR have Higgsfield compose it as one piece.
AI-generated imagery of other pros (Musty, AppJack, Kinseh, Wayton, Squishy, Jynxzi) — Luke's face only
Pure text-on-color ads — always pair text with an RL scene element
